research_question: BTC spot alpha beyond VTREND trend-following?
incumbent: VTREND E5+EMA21D1 (full-range: Sh 1.30, 217 trades; eval-window: Sh 1.43, 186 trades)

1_audit_state_map.method: v10 BacktestEngine (next-bar-open fills, harsh cost 50 bps RT)

2_flat_eda.method: H4/H1/D1 conditional analysis, gapped ACF, per-period VR, R/S Hurst, Spearman, Kruskal-Wallis

4_formalization.method: Decision-theoretic formalization, bar-level conditional returns, power analysis, MI estimation
4_formalization.key_finding: Bar-level ρ(1) = -0.054 (vs per-period VR-implied -0.238). Gross 2.9 bps/trade, 17x below 50 bps cost. All 3 classes UNDERPOWERED. MI ≈ 2 millibits.

observations.Obs02: Flat duration right-skewed: median 1.8d, mean 8.2d, max 103d
observations.Obs03: Longer flat periods tend to have more negative returns (Phase 0 finding)
observations.Obs04: Flat bars: mean -8.15 bps, vol 163 bps; active: +14.47 bps, vol 125 bps (Phase 0)
observations.Obs06: Flat returns year-dependent; medians near zero, means pulled by drawdowns (Phase 0)
observations.Obs07: IN_TRADE median 28 bars (4.7d), IQR 14-50 bars; consistent with known ~29 bars
observations.Obs08: Bear years ~23-25% exposure, bull years ~54-58%; D1 EMA(21) filter is primary driver
observations.Obs09: 49.5% of trades begin within 10 bars (40h) of previous exit
observations.Obs11: FLAT mean -2.05 bps, IN_TRADE +6.23 bps; extreme tails in FLAT
observations.Obs13: FLAT skew -0.126, less negative than IN_TRADE -0.322
observations.Obs14: FLAT return ACF: lag 1 = -0.052, lag 6 = -0.076 (24h cycle); 15/50 lags sig
observations.Obs15: FLAT |return| ACF: lag 1 = 0.278, all 50 lags significant (strong vol clustering)
observations.Obs17: Per-period VR << 1 (VR(2)=0.82, VR(20)=0.72, all p<0.0001) — mean-reversion
observations.Obs18: Hurst mixed (0.51 concat, 0.62 longest); VR per-period more informative
observations.Obs19: FLAT vol HIGHER than IN_TRADE (62.5% vs 53.0%)
observations.Obs20: Vol within flat: elevated mid-period (68.7%), lower at boundaries (54-56%)
observations.Obs21: No predictive power: flat characteristics → next trade return (all |ρ|<0.07, p>0.35)
observations.Obs22: Pre-entry drift +1.03% (expected for trend entry); no win/loss distinction
observations.Obs23: Post-exit: +0.81% after winners (trend continues), -0.02% after losers
observations.Obs24: Post-exit vol elevated at exit, decays over ~5 bars
observations.Obs25: H1 reproduces H4: lag-24 ACF = -0.041, |ret| ACF 0.321, VR confirms mean-reversion
observations.Obs26: D1 FLAT: Hurst 0.467, mean -18.43 bps, skew -1.586 (mean-reverting at daily)
observations.Obs27: Mean-reversion consistent across H1/H4/D1 — not timeframe-specific
observations.Obs28: 3 calendar effects significant + STABLE: hourly return, intraday vol, weekend vol
observations.Obs29: All 3 stable across half-sample splits; intraday vol pattern is strongest

propositions.Prop01: Mean-reversion exists but is economically negligible (bar-level ρ=-0.054, gross +2.9 bps, p=0.216) [HIGH]
propositions.Prop02: No function class can overcome cost barrier (gross 3-9 bps vs 15-50 bps cost) [HIGH]
propositions.Prop03: Preceding flat-period VR has zero predictive power for trade quality (ρ=0.053, p=0.515) [HIGH]
propositions.Prop04: Mean-reversion weakens/reverses at longer horizons (k=20: -27.9 bps, p=0.010) [MEDIUM]
propositions.Prop05: All three admissible classes are UNDERPOWERED (|d|/MDE = 0.23 to 0.92) [HIGH]

figures.Fig08: figures/Fig08_scatter_matrix.png — Flat characteristics vs next trade return
figures.Fig11: figures/Fig11_cross_timeframe_acf.png — H1 vs H4 ACF comparison
figures.Fig12: figures/Fig12_rolling_stability.png — Rolling VR(2), ACF(6), η² stability
